rulings.Case Title: MAIN STREET ACQUISITION CORP VS. BITAH A HONARMAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 19, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MAIN STREET ACQUISITION CORP's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Hearing required. Judgment Debtor Bitah H. Honarmand must trace the levied funds to an exempt source. (CCP § 703.080(b).) In the claim of exemption, Judgment Debtor states that the Bank of America account is used only for direct deposit of Social Security benefits, and Judgment Debtor transferred these funds from Bank of America to the Wells Fargo Bank account that is currently levied. However, in the Bank of America Account Activity, a transfer was made on March 12, 2014 from a savings account to the Bank of America account. Judgment Debtor has not demonstrated that the levied funds are traceable to an exempt source. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf, Jane Doe, To Provide Further Answers To Deposition Questions And Produce Documents; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, May 19, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT TIMOTHY MARTEN's Motion To Compel Plaintiff, Jane Doe, To Provide Further Answers To Deposition Questions And Produce Documents; Request For Monetary Sanctions (Tentative ruling is split into 2 parts; Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Geoff Howard, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants move for further responses to 11 deposition questions, for production of a document referenced in the deposition, and for monetary sanctions. Plaintiff's deposition has taken place over 5 days so far. The record contains long colloquy between counsel, numerous speaking objections and instructions not to answer on a variety of grounds. Defendants waited until the last day to file this motion without engaging in meet and confer other than what had occurred on the record at the deposition. Plaintiff contends the motion is untimely and that defendants failed adequately to meet and confer. The Court finds that the motion is timely as it was filed within 60 days of defendant receiving the transcript. The Court also finds that although defendants should have engaged in further meet and confer, the parties did meet and confer on the issues during the course of the deposition. Accordingly, the Court will not deny the motion for failure to meet and confer this time, but expects the parties to engage in meaningful meet and confer well in advance of any motion deadlines in the future (outside the deposition). Turning to the substance, the court grants in part and denies in part defendants' motion as follows: Question 1 is denied. Defendants have made an insufficient showing of relevance. Question 2 is granted. Plaintiff did not fully answer the question. Question 3 is granted. It is fair cross-examination. Question 4 is denied. The issue is more appropriate for a document request. Question 5 is denied. Plaintiff answered the question. Question 6 is granted. Plaintiff can give her best lay answer, if she has one. Question 7 is denied on relevance and privacy grounds. Question 8 is denied on relevance grounds and lack of apparent foundation. Question 9 is granted. The question is fair and plaintiff has not provided an answer to it but rather to a different question. Question 10 is denied. The general subject matter is covered elsewhere in the deposition. Motions for sanctions are denied. The plaintiff shall appear for further deposition within 25 days of this order for questioning limited to Questions 2, 3, 6 and 9 and reasonable, directly related, follow-up questions. Total record time shall not exceed 20 minutes. (continued to line #2)=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROY SHIMEK, AN INDIVIDUAL et al VS. DAVID KARNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 19, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTs DAVID KARNER and MICHAEL ROCHE's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The court grants the anti-SLAPP motion to strike. The declaration of Mary Wiederhold establishes that she contacted Ms. Zahner to investigate the wrongful eviction and see whether others had complained about the property. Under Dove Audio, Inc. v. Rosenfeld, Meyer & Sussman (1996), 47 Cal.App.4th 777, 781, "communications made during an attorney's investigatory interviews with private individuals preparatory to a hearing" implicate the anti-SLAPP statute and the litigation privilege. The burden has shifted to plaintiffs to demonstrate a probability of prevailing. Plaintiffs fail to meet their burden. First, plaintiff merely rely on the declaration of counsel and fail to show that the statement was even uttered. Second, assuming that Ms. Wiederhold made the statement, it is privileged under CC 47 and Dove Audio. Third, assuming that the statement is one of fact, the only evidence before the court demonstrates that it is true. The declaration of Mr. Karner cites the habitability issues and the premises is fairly characterized as a "hovel." The court grants $5,652.50 in fees and costs to defendants Karner and Roche. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

The court grants the anti-SLAPP motion to strike. The declaration of Mary Wiederhold establishes that she contacted Ms. Zahner to investigate the wrongful eviction and see whether others had complained about the property. Under Dove Audio, Inc. v. Rosenfeld, Meyer & Sussman (1996), 47 Cal.App.4th 777, 781, "communications made during an attorney's investigatory interviews with private individuals preparatory to a hearing" implicate the anti-SLAPP statute and the litigation privilege. The burden has shifted to plaintiffs to demonstrate a probability of prevailing. Plaintiffs fail to meet their burden. First, plaintiff merely rely on the declaration of counsel and fail to show that the statement was even uttered. Second, assuming that Ms. Wiederhold made the statement, it is privileged under CC 47 and Dove Audio. Third, assuming that the statement is one of fact, the only evidence before the court demonstrates that it is true. The declaration of Mr. Karner cites the habitability issues and the premises is fairly characterized as a "hovel." Defendant Wiederhold may seek her fees by motion.
